reading: Moderate environmental action can improve transport when coherent motion is trapped by interference or disorder.
Dephasing can assist transport in networks, but excessive dephasing suppresses useful coherent motion.
Efficiency scans over disorder and dephasing are standard diagnostics for environment-assisted transport.
Disorder can assist in suboptimal decoherence regimes, so ensemble statistics are necessary.
Trap position and graph topology strongly affect continuous-time quantum-walk transport efficiency.
Quantum simulators observe a progression from coherent dynamics to assistance and high-noise suppression.
Recent experiments support intermediate-regime transport where static disorder and dephasing compete.
